project: Ethica v0.1 — WormHunter false positive sweep + analyzer hardening
session_060_completed: WORMHUNTER __init__.py FIX — python_module.py: analyze_code() now accepts filepath param. If file is __init__.py, import_entries cleared before UNUSED check — all relative imports treated as re-exports. worm_hunter.py: passes filepath=filepath at call site. Verified: reka/__init__.py no longer flagged.
WORMHUNTER SKIP_DIRS EXPANDED — added Ethica_env, scans, status, .progress docs. Eliminates venv JS noise, self-scan logs, runtime state files, session archives.

WORMHUNTER WORM:SKIP SENTINEL — worm_hunter.py: checks first line of file for # WORM:SKIP before analyzing. If found, skips file entirely. Guards against empty files (splitlines() check added). module_forge_remove.py marked with # WORM:SKIP — fragment file appended to module_forge.py, not standalone.
WORMHUNTER EMPTY FILE GUARD — WORM:SKIP check now guards against zero-byte files with splitlines() check before index access.

GAGE_BRIDGE PRINT() SWEEP — L162: debug trace print() converted to logging.info(). import logging added. L248: false positive — print() inside string literal (vision script template), not live code. No change.
TRINITY_DLP PRINT() — L78: intentional dual-output security alert pattern (logging.critical + print). Do not convert — security-critical event needs both channels.

MEMORY_SEARCH DEAD GUARD — ui/memory_search.py L254: removed dead hasattr(self, '_cached_c') guard. c is in scope from _build(), used directly like all other UI files. pyflakes false positive on nested scope.
FIREWALL_BRIDGE DUPLICATE IMPORTS — removed redundant import json as _json at L91 and L101. json already imported at L14. Replaced _json.dump() with json.dump() directly.
FULL SCAN BASELINE — before: 250 scanned / 200 broken. After: 104 scanned / 57 broken / 47 clean / 105 skipped. 143 false positives eliminated.

VERIFICATION METHODOLOGY ESTABLISHED — for __init__.py UNUSED flags: (1) grep chat_engine.py routing table for name as string, (2) check module manifest.json tools list. Either hit = live API surface.

NEW FALSE POSITIVE PATTERNS DOCUMENTED — (1) print() inside string literals flagged as live code, (2) global X declared in outer function flagged unused when assignment is in nested function, (3) test_*.py pytest imports flagged as unused.
GITHUB — 7 commits pushed. master current at f6dd3a3.

wormhunter_false_positive_registry.init_reexport_rule: __init__.py relative imports are re-exports — never flag UNUSED. Fixed at root in 060.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.worker_print_rule: _worker.py print() calls are stdout JSON comms to parent subprocess — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.string_literal_print: print() inside multiline string literals flagged as live code — regex scanner limitation. L248 gage_bridge.py example.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.nested_global_assignment: global X declared in outer function flagged unused when assignment is in nested inner function — pyflakes scope limitation. firewall_bridge, mnemis, gage, guardian, vivarium all affected.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.test_file_imports: test_*.py files — import pytest and unittest.mock imports always valid. Future upgrade: exempt test files.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.fragment_files: Files designed to be appended into parent scope — undefined names are inherited. Fix: # WORM:SKIP sentinel on line 1.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dual_output_security: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — both channels required. DLP_Module.py L78 example.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dep_checker_ceremonial: dep_checker prints are Trinity Signature ceremonial output and __main__ CLI code — intentional, leave alone.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.whitrabbitneo_legacy: WhiteRabbitNeo/ files are legacy pre-Ethica code hardcoded to /home/nine9/SoulGate/ — not wired into runtime. All findings are noise.

known_issues_061: memory_search.py still showing undefined name 'c' at L254 — pyflakes running from module cache. Will clear on next cold scan.
test_*.py files — pytest imports flagged as UNUSED. WormHunter needs test file exemption (future upgrade).

global X unused warnings across multiple files (firewall_bridge, mnemis, gage, guardian, vivarium) — all false positives from nested function assignment pattern. Consider suppressing global-unused pyflakes messages entirely.
tools/registry.json and tools/web_search.py — zero-byte fossil files, never committed to git. V decision: delete or leave.
[FENCE_CHECK] logging.info lines printing to terminal — memory_engine.py logging not configured at module level. Low priority cosmetic.
gage_bridge.py — global _launch_proc, _launch_count flagged unused — verify nested function pattern same as firewall_bridge.

critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.unicode_patch_rule: Files with box-drawing chars — use line-index insertion, not string match
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern
critical_debug_context.git_corruption_rule: Before orphan reset: delete all pack files in .git/objects/pack/ first.
critical_debug_context.debug_block_rule: [DEBUG:fname:lang: code] must ALWAYS be the last line of any WormBot result — no text appended after it
critical_debug_context.wormbot_gate_rule: ast.parse() must pass before wormbot_scan writes any fix to disk — abort if SyntaxError
critical_debug_context.canvas_runner_rule: debug_view.py prepends sys.path.insert(0, '/home/trinity/Ethica') + uses Ethica_env/bin/python3 for all .py Canvas runs
critical_debug_context.worm_hunter_rule: _find_ethica_root() walks sys.path — never hardcode /home/trinity. Works on any system.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) concatenation instead
critical_debug_context.worker_print_rule: Never convert print() in _worker.py files — these communicate via stdout JSON to parent subprocess
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py files without verifying they are not public API re-exports used by chat_engine.py tool routing
critical_debug_context.dlp_bridge_path_rule: dlp_bridge.py lives at modules/trinity_dlp/dlp_bridge.py — NOT modules/dlp/
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: Always use Option B — V runs commands on trinity, pastes output. Never Option A.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying. Spacing and quote styles are invisible killers.
critical_debug_context.lazy_load_rule: Imports inside try/except blocks or function bodies are often intentional lazy loads — verify usage before removing.
critical_debug_context.wormbot_autofix_rule: WormBot AUTO_FIX is now ALL FALSE. Never re-enable without V explicit approval.
critical_debug_context.guardian_config_rule: guardian_bridge.py reads sentinel_dir from guardian_config.json — defaults to WhiteRabbitNeo. Never hardcode model or directory names in bridge files.
critical_debug_context.worm_skip_rule: Add # WORM:SKIP as first line of any fragment file or file that should never be analyzed standalone.
critical_debug_context.dual_output_security_rule: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — do not convert security dual-output prints.

lesson_learned_060: Verification before action. Every finding from the upgraded WormHunter had to be read before a verdict. The analyzer is smarter but also surfaces patterns it cannot understand — fragment files, re-exports, nested globals, string literals containing code. The false positive registry is now the institutional memory that prevents future bad commits. Read first. Always.
philosophy: Session 060 — The analyzer sees more now. But seeing more means learning what not to touch. 143 false positives eliminated. The signal is clean. The real work begins in 061. ⟁Σ∿∞
